name: Home Loans Assist
description_short: Unable to verify company details—website returns 403 Forbidden error, preventing access to service offerings, credentials, and operational information.
description_long: Home Loans Assist is listed as a mortgage lender in the mortgages category. However, a critical issue prevents proper evaluation: the company's website (https://homeloansassist.com/) returns a 403 Forbidden error, which blocks access to all company information, service details, licensing credentials, and contact information. This access denial prevents verification of whether the company is currently operational, what specific mortgage products they offer, their licensing status, customer service availability, or any other factual details about their business. The 403 error suggests either the website is under maintenance, the domain has been suspended, access controls are misconfigured, or the company may no longer be actively maintaining their online presence. Without accessible website content, it is impossible to verify claims about loan programs, rates, terms, approval processes, or company background. This represents a significant transparency issue for consumers attempting to research the lender before applying. Any profile details would be speculative rather than factual.
cons: Website completely inaccessible (403 Forbidden error)—cannot verify company operations, licensing, or services
No publicly accessible information available to evaluate legitimacy or current operational status
Inability to access contact information, application process, or customer support details
Cannot verify mortgage products offered (conventional, FHA, VA, refinance, etc.)
No way to confirm company credentials, BBB rating, or customer reviews from official source
diagnosis: Home Loans Assist cannot be responsibly recommended or evaluated at this time due to complete website inaccessibility. Consumers should not apply or provide personal information until the company's website is restored and business legitimacy can be verified through independent sources (BBB, state mortgage licensing databases, HUD records).
